channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Nigerian neurosurgeon sparks brain drain debate after US hospital appointment
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Dr. Salamat Aliu-Ibrahim’s appointment in the US sparks debate on Nigeria’s brain drain. Discover reactions to the neurosurgeon’s career move.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerian-neurosurgeon-sparks-brain-drain-debate-after-us-hospital-appointment/

item.title: AEDC begins repairs after resolving Maitama substation crisis
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
AEDC has commenced repairs on the damaged C2 Maitama Substation, restoring power to affected areas after resolving invasion issues. Learn more about the AE
Read More: https://punchng.com/aedc-begins-repairs-after-resolving-maitama-substation-crisis/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Reps warn agencies shunning summons
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives issues a final warning to agencies shunning summons in the PFIPC scandal probe, threatening constitutional powers to compel ap
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-reps-warn-agencies-shunning-summons/

item.title: SGF reaffirms Nigeria’s commitment to stronger ties with Serbia
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s SGF reaffirms commitment to strengthening diplomatic and economic ties with Serbia. Discover how both nations plan to boost trade and investment.
Read More: https://punchng.com/sgf-reaffirms-nigerias-commitment-to-stronger-ties-with-serbia/

item.title: Oyo sachet water producers propose recycling scheme over total ban
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Oyo sachet water producers urge reconsideration of proposed ban, warning of economic impact and job losses. They propose a recycling scheme to address plas
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyo-sachet-water-producers-propose-recycling-scheme-over-total-ban/

item.title: FG urges AU to crack down on illicit terror funding
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s FG calls on the African Union to strengthen efforts against illicit financial flows, a major driver of terrorism and violent extremism across Afr
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-urges-au-to-crack-down-on-illicit-terror-funding/

item.title: Gunmen kill four herders, set two houses ablaze in Anambra
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Four herders were killed and two houses set ablaze in a gruesome attack by gunmen in Agu-Ifite, Awka, Anambra State. Police have launched a manhunt for the
Read More: https://punchng.com/gunmen-kill-four-herders-set-two-houses-ablaze-in-anambra/

item.title: FG issues flood alert for 17 states
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian government has issued a flood alert for 17 states, warning of medium flood risk. Learn which states are affected and what precautions to take.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-issues-flood-alert-for-17-states/

item.title: SGF urges Code of Conduct Tribunal to embrace technology
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
SGF George Akume urges the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT) to embrace digital technology for improved case management and operational efficiency in justice
Read More: https://punchng.com/sgf-urges-code-of-conduct-tribunal-to-embrace-technology/

item.title: Stones emerges as potential Chelsea target
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Free agent defender John Stones has emerged as a potential option for Chelsea in this transfer window.

item.title: Police arrest suspected hoodlum over Osun NURTW clash
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Police in Osun State have arrested a suspected hoodlum following a violent NURTW clash in Osogbo that left three security operatives injured. Read more her
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-suspected-hoodlum-over-osun-nurtw-clash/

item.title: FG, AMMON Settle Dispute, Clear Path For 1.55m Smart Meters
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Millions of Nigerian electricity consumers could soon be spared estimated billing after the Federal Government resolved the legal dispute that stalled the procurement of 1.55 million smart meters. The resolution followed the withdrawal of a lawsuit by the Association of Meter Manufacturers of Nigeri

item.title: BREAKING: Gunmen invade Anambra community, kill four, raze houses
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Although the police did not specifically identify the victims, sources informed PREMIUM TIMES that the victims were herders residing at the settlement. The post BREAKING: Gunmen invade Anambra community, kill four, raze houses appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Africa now world’s hunger epicentre, with 309 million hungry people – Report
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Africa has become the world's hunger epicentre, having an estimated 309 million hungry people, with countries such as Nigeria facing The post Africa now world’s hunger epicentre, with 309 million hungry people – Report appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau (NSIB) and the Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency (NIMASA) have agreed to establish a joint framework for data sharing, safety investigations and technical cooperation aimed at strengthening maritime safety across Nigeria’s waterw

item.title: Watch: Burna Boy and Harry Kane spotted together in New York in unexpected encounter
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Bayern Munich star Harry Kane was spotted dancing with Nigerian Afrobreats star Burna Boy at a club in New York on Monday, Soccernet.ng reports. The 2026 FIFA World Cup has ended, and most players who were involved with their countries have already gone on breaks, as they look to wind off and blow o

item.title: Fresh crisis hits Ondo Assembly over alleged sharing of ₦44 million OSOPADEC funds
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Some members of the House are reportedly calling for the resignation of the Speaker, Olamide Oladiji, for cornering the money, a claim the speaker denied. The post Fresh crisis hits Ondo Assembly over alleged sharing of ₦44 million OSOPADEC funds appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Reps Demand Audit Of Recovered Assets Since 1999
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives Tuesday called for a comprehensive audit of all assets seized, forfeited, recovered, managed, disposed of or repatriated by government institutions since the country’s return to democratic rule in 1999. The call was consequent upon the adoption of a motion sponsore

item.title: “It’s confusing” — CAF Ambassador Eni Aluko questions decision to overturn controversial AFCON final result
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: CAF ambassador and former England international Eni Aluko has become the latest person to question the Confederation of African Football's controversial decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations title and award it to Morocco, Soccernet.ng reports. The ruling, announced months after

item.title: Tinubu Lifts Akwete Fabric Cooperative With N2bn
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Wife of the President, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, has empowered the Akwete cloth women cooperative with N2billion. She said that after campaign and election, real governance begins. “This is part of governing. I don’t know anything about Akwete. I believe my visit here is divine. I was watc

item.title: 'They want us gone' - Man Utd fans' fury over ticket ultimatum
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Fans tell BBC Sport that a crackdown on touting is affecting some of the club's most loyal fans.

item.title: An oil lifeline is under threat, and markets have yet to price in the growing crisis
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The Houthis escalated threats on Tuesday, risking millions of barrels of Saudi oil that had been reaching energy markets despite the U.S.-Iran war.

item.title: Tinubu to NBA President: Correct issues that arose during your election
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The President also urged her to reach out to the other contestants, unify the bar, and make efforts to correct issues that arose during her election. The post Tinubu to NBA President: Correct issues that arose during your election appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Unilever Nigeria raises interim dividend fourfold amid slight increase in half-year profit
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: As much as N11.5 billion, a 73.7 per cent equivalent of post-tax profit, will be allocated to equity investors for an accounting period in which the profit itself only grew by a single digit. The post Unilever Nigeria raises interim dividend fourfold amid slight increase in half-year profit appeared

item.title: FG Receives Report On $500m AGROW Programme, Set To Begin Implementation
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Government has received the final report of the National Technical Working Group on the World Bank-supported $500 million Sustainable Agricultural Value Chains for Growth Programme (AGROW), marking the conclusion of the programme’s design phase and its transition to implementation.

item.title: La Liga boss Tebas calls for Infantino’s resignation, says FIFA harming football
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: La Liga president Javier Tebas has called for FIFA president Gianni Infantino to step down, accusing the world football governing body of taking decisions that are damaging the game and undermining domestic football competitions. The post La Liga boss Tebas calls for Infantino’s resignation, s

item.title: Tinubu’s Reforms Will Strengthen Nigeria’s Economy Despite Short-Term Pain – Outgoing British Envoy
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Outgoing British High Commissioner to Nigeria, Richard Montgomery, has commended President Bola Tinubu’s economic reforms, describing them as fundamental steps that will place Nigeria on a stronger economic footing despite the short-term hardships experienced by citizens. Montgomery made the remarks

item.title: Top 10 Samsung phones for students on a budget
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: As the cost of living continues to rise, students are increasingly turning to affordable Samsung smartphones that can handle both academic and everyday needs without stretching their budgets. The post Top 10 Samsung phones for students on a budget appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: South Africa Returnees Find Shelter At OPM, Seek Government Support
item.dc:creator: Channels TV

The post South Africa Returnees Find Shelter At OPM, Seek Government Support appeared first on Channels T

item.title: Yiaga Africa flags voter inducement, rising tension ahead of Osun election
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: An election observation group, Yiaga Africa, has raised concerns about alleged voter inducement and growing tension ahead of the August 15 Osun State governorship election. The post Yiaga Africa flags voter inducement, rising tension ahead of Osun election appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Broad: England Test job less attractive than it was
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Former England bowler Stuart Broad says he understands Andy Flower's decision to turn down the England Test job, and is unsure over who the ECB turn to next, pondering whether the role has become "less attractive".

item.title: ‘I have a $2,000 deductible’: I reported a cracked windshield on my Hertz rental car. Can they charge me?

item.description: “I never heard a rock hit the windshield.”

item.description: The Nigeria Hydrological Services Agency (NIHSA) has warned that 17 states are at risk of flooding between July 21 and 27, urging residents and authorities in affected areas to take immediate precautionary measures. item.title: Gaza Celebrates Spain’s World Cup Victory
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown. Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied th

item.description: The Minister of Defence, General Christopher Musa, rtd, has said that he would take a legal action against whoever said he resigned from his position. item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The House of Representatives has resolved to investigate the human and economic losses suffered by Nigerians during repeated xenophobic attacks in South Africa. The resolution followed the adoption of a motion sponsored by the member representing Ikorodu Federal Constituency of Lagos State, Babajimi

item.title: Show HN: A self-running space economy SIM in Rust and Bevy

item.title: Xenophobia: Reps Move To Document Losses, Seek Compensation For Nigerians In South Africa
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Lawmakers argued that a thorough assessment of the impact of the attacks is necessary to support affected Nigerians and strengthen the country’s response to future incidents. The post Xenophobia: Reps Move To Document Losses, Seek Compensation For Nigerians In South Africa appeared first on Channels

item.title: Resignation Claims: Defence Minister threatens legal action, denies resigning
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Christopher Musa, Nigeria’s Minister of Defence, has firmly dismissed recent online rumors claiming he was stepping down, threatening to take read more Resignation Claims: Defence Minister threatens legal action, denies resigning

item.title: CREDICORP Launches C.L.I.C.K.D, a Device Financing Scheme with FG’s 3MTT Programme
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: …expanding affordable consumer credit for locally-assembled laptops and devices for digital workers As part of its mission to democratize access to consumer credit, expand economic opportunity and empower millions of Nigerians to improve their quality of life through responsible borrowing, the Niger

item.title: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.

item.title: Resignation claims: Defence minister meets Tinubu, threatens legal action, declares, “I’m going nowhere”
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Minister of Defence General Christopher Musa (retd.), on Tuesday, dismissed reports that he had resigned and promised to sue those responsible for spreading the rumours after a private briefing with President Bola Tinubu at Aso Rock Villa. The post Resignation claims: Defence minister meets Tinubu,

item.title: 8,588 teachers appointed to strengthen Kano education sector
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kano State Governor, Alhaji Abba Kabir Yusuf, has presented appointment letters to 8,588 teachers as part of efforts to boost educational development in the state. The governor made the remarks during the official presentation of appointment letters to 4,900 newly recruited teachers and 3,688 ABBA G

item.title: Nigeria, Germany set to hold bilateral talks
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The German minister is on his third trip to Africa this year. The post Nigeria, Germany set to hold bilateral talks appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

item.title: Embrace Digital Technology In Justice Dispensation, SGF Tells CCT
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF), Senator George Akume, has charged the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT) to embrace digital technology in the administration of justice to enhance case management, records administration and overall operational efficiency. The SGF gave the charge

item.title: Rebecca’s lawyers petition UN, EU, US over alleged EFCC rights violations
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: In the petitions, the legal team stressed that they were not asking the international bodies to interfere with any criminal investigation or determine ownership of the disputed property.

item.title: SGF Receives Serbian Envoy, Reaffirms Commitment To Strengthen Bilateral Ties
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF), Senator George Akume, has reaffirmed the Federal Government’s commitment to strengthening the longstanding diplomatic and economic ties between Nigeria and the Republic of Serbia. In a statement by Chris Ugwuegbulam, Head of the Informat

item.title: Trump looks set to roll out a new set of tariffs. Will markets care?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The president is expected to impose fresh import taxes this week, as levies from February are only able to remain in effect for 150 days.

item.title: Davido, 30BG In Attendance As Israel DMW Laid Father To Rest In Benin
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigerian Afrobeats singer, David Adeleke, better known as Davido’s logistic manager, Israel DMW has penned a heartfelt message to family, friends and fans following the burial ceremony of his father. New Telegraph had in March 2026, reported that Israel DMW lost his father to the cold hand of
